prompt:
A short 10 cm length of 22 AWG stranded jumper wire lies on a desk, with a neat soldered splice visible in the middle and a snug piece of 2 mm heat-shrink tubing shrunk over the joint. The insulation on either side of the splice is a solid color, and the heat-shrink segment is slightly darker and smoother, clearly reinforcing the repaired or extended connection. A soldering iron and a small spool of heat-shrink tubing appear in the background, making it obvious that this is a carefully soldered jumper rather than a bare twist.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics workspace with soft, even lighting, tidy bins of wire and components, and a plant or two in the blurred distance. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on tools, and a composition that centers the wire, splice, and heat-shrink while the rest of the bench and lab equipment remains softly out of focus.
